zh.Markham Clinton, Nottinghamshire, Englan: 英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾馬卡姆 克林頓
zh.lifeSummary: Peter de Foun是Henry Foun or Foune的兒子，於大約1275年出生在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾馬卡姆 克林頓。他於1299年與Margaret de Santa Cruce在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.Markham Clinton, Nottinghamshire, Englan: 英国英格兰諾廷加姆爾馬卡姆 克林頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Peter de Foun是Henry Foun or Foune的儿子，于大约1275年出生在英国英格兰諾廷加姆爾馬卡姆 克林頓。他于1299年与Margaret de Santa Cruce在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Foun
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Arabic (mainly Lebanon and Maghreb): from a French-influenced variant of a personal name based on the Arabic noun ʿaun ‘help, aid’. In Lebanon it is common among Christians. Compare Aun 1.

variants.name: Aun
meanings: Muslim (Pakistan): from a personal name based on Arabic ʿaun ‘help, aid’. Compare Aoun .
Cambodian: written អូន, same as   Oun   2.
Estonian: arbitrary surname from aun ‘long stack of peat, crops or hay’ or õun ‘apple’, as the character õ was often substituted with a in the old spelling.

nameMeanings.name: Peter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

en.lifeSummary: Peter de Foun was born about 1275, in Markham Clinton, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom as the son of Henry Foun or Foune. He married Margaret de Santa Cruce in 1299, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.

ko.lifeSummary: Peter de Foun(은)는 대략 1275년 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 마카 클린턴에서 Henry Foun or Foune의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1299년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Margaret de Santa Cruce(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.
